title: Nothing Works and Everyone Is Euphoric
excerpt: As I’m writing this, we’re in the middle of an AI-induced mass psychosis. People are literally token-maxxing themselves into hospital beds, scrambling to capture some of that market value before everything is automated away. I can’t blame them. Models keep getting better, programmers are being laid off left and right. We’ve been repeatedly told that AI will write 100% of the code by the end of the year. Whether that’s true or not, this may not be the best time to sit back.
content_html:
As I’m writing this, we’re in the middle of an AI-induced mass psychosis. People are literally token-maxxing themselves into hospital beds, scrambling to capture some of that market value before everything is automated away. I can’t blame them. Models keep getting better, programmers are being laid off left and right. We’ve been repeatedly told that AI will write 100% of the code by the end of the year. Whether that’s true or not, this may not be the best time to sit back.

The widespread excitement around the Agentic Era comes with the promise of greater productivity and higher quality. There’s no denying that these new tools have already revolutionized how we create and use software. They have raised upper management’s expectations for team output. They may have upgraded the average skill set of software teams in a way we have not seen before.

So why does software keep getting worse across the board?

A few examples from last week alone:

  • My banking app requires, on average, three FaceID logins before the 3D Secure confirmation view appears.
  • I opened Slack on macOS, the icon kept bouncing in the dock for a few seconds. I got impatient, switched to Ghostty, and started typing. Just then, the Slack window appeared, stole focus from Ghostty and the git pull command was sent to the group chat.
  • My LG fridge started making weird sounds, so I tried to file a warranty claim, through a multi-step form with countless fields. It failed with a submission error at the very end. And I only found out because I looked at the JavaScript console.
  • My car’s infotainment system got a software update recently. It was never great to begin with, but at least it didn’t reboot itself during every drive. Now, it’s riddled with bugs: the turn-signal sound randomly goes silent until I reboot the OS; I tap the screen to open Google Maps – the radio app shows up; there’s a 1-2-second lag before anything happens after I tap the screen. This is no longer just a UX problem at this point – those bugs affect your ability to focus on driving.

    A few months ago, I saw a LinkedIn thread by a PM on the team that redesigned the car’s OS. They were congratulating themselves on what an amazing job they had done. I keep thinking about that post every time I have to fight their product.

While I can’t know the full story, I’m willing to bet that most of the teams behind those bugs have access to the latest models, with generous token budgets. LLMs can be really good at squashing bugs if given the chance.

Software has always had bugs, and the nostalgia for the good old macOS Snow Leopard era when everything was stable is mostly the product of selective memory. Software may have been better back in the day, but that was mainly because it was much simpler. Since then, we have kept coming up with new abstractions, new frontend frameworks, and more infrastructure complexity. The bar for “user experience” has kept rising, but everything has become increasingly fragile.

We’ve reached a point where an update to macOS – or to any app I rely on, really – is a source of dread rather than excitement. I now expect the new version to be worse.

This isn’t a rant against AI. Those humming GPU farms have given us superpowers, but we still can’t use them to build better software.

Software vendors have long been KPI-oriented, and making things more stable doesn’t always have a direct effect on the numbers. It doesn’t look exciting in presentations:

This quarter, we won’t be releasing any new features, and we have no plans to redesign anything — we will exclusively focus on fixing bugs.

— Imaginary PM at a BigCo

Until this attitude changes, the great software quality decay will continue.

That doesn’t sound optimistic, but I’m actually excited about what comes next. As companies collectively spiral into AI debt, individual developers have a unique opportunity to build software that would previously have been beyond their reach.

I have no hope for my car’s Android Auto or LG’s stupid website – but I choose to believe that everyday software will get better as a result of this frustration. We’re already seeing acts of rebellion against the current state of macOS and Windows, and I hope the trend will spread across the stack.

sentences.zh: 我的 LG 冰箱开始发出奇怪的声音，因此我尝试通过包含无数字段的多步骤表格提交保修索赔。

sentences.en: It failed with a submission error at the very end.
sentences.zh: 最后因提交错误而失败。

sentences.en: And I only found out because I looked at the JavaScript console.

blocks.html_en:
• My car’s infotainment system got a software update recently. It was never great to begin with, but at least it didn’t reboot itself during every drive. Now, it’s riddled with bugs: the turn-signal sound randomly goes silent until I reboot the OS; I tap the screen to open Google Maps – the radio app shows up; there’s a 1-2-second lag before anything happens after I tap the screen. sentences.en: My car’s infotainment system got a software update recently.
sentences.zh: 我汽车的信息娱乐系统最近进行了软件更新。

sentences.en: It was never great to begin with, but at least it didn’t reboot itself during every drive.
sentences.zh: 一开始它就不是很好，但至少它不会在每次驾驶过程中自行重新启动。

sentences.en: Now, it’s riddled with bugs: the turn-signal sound randomly goes silent until I reboot the OS; I tap the screen to open Google Maps – the radio app shows up; there’s a 1-2-second lag before anything happens after I tap the screen.
sentences.zh: 现在，它充满了错误：转向灯的声音随机变得安静，直到我重新启动操作系统；我点击屏幕打开 Google 地图 – 收音机应用程序出现；点击屏幕后，有 1-2 秒的延迟才会发生任何事情。

sentences.en: This is no longer just a UX problem at this point – those bugs affect your ability to focus on driving.
sentences.zh: 此时，这不再只是一个用户体验问题 - 这些错误会影响您专注于驾驶的能力。

sentences.en: A few months ago, I saw a LinkedIn thread by a PM on the team that redesigned the car’s OS.
sentences.zh: 几个月前，我在 LinkedIn 上看到了重新设计汽车操作系统的团队的一位产品经理的帖子。

sentences.en: They were congratulating themselves on what an amazing job they had done.
sentences.zh: 他们祝贺自己所做的工作多么出色。

sentences.en: I keep thinking about that post every time I have to fight their product.
sentences.zh: 每次我不得不与他们的产品作斗争时，我都会想起那篇文章。

sentences.en: LLMs can be really good at squashing bugs if given the chance.
sentences.zh: 如果有机会的话，法学硕士可以非常擅长消除错误。

The bar for “user experience” has kept rising, but everything has become increasingly fragile.

sentences.en: Software has always had bugs, and the nostalgia for the good old macOS Snow Leopard era when everything was stable is mostly the product of selective memory.
sentences.zh: 软件总是有缺陷的，对一切都很稳定的 macOS Snow Leopard 时代的怀念主要是选择性记忆的产物。

sentences.en: Software may have been better back in the day, but that was mainly because it was much simpler.
sentences.zh: 过去的软件可能更好，但这主要是因为它更简单。

sentences.en: Since then, we have kept coming up with new abstractions, new frontend frameworks, and more infrastructure complexity.
sentences.zh: 从那时起，我们不断提出新的抽象、新的前端框架和更多的基础设施复杂性。

sentences.en: The bar for “user experience” has kept rising, but everything has become increasingly fragile.
sentences.zh: “用户体验”的门槛不断提高，但一切都变得越来越脆弱。

blocks.html_en: We’ve reached a point where an update to macOS – or to any app I rely on, really – is a source of dread rather than excitement. I now expect the new version to be worse.

sentences.en: We’ve reached a point where an update to macOS – or to any app I rely on, really – is a source of dread rather than excitement.
sentences.zh: 我们已经到了这样一个地步：macOS（或者我真正依赖的任何应用程序）的更新让人感到恐惧而不是兴奋。

sentences.en: I now expect the new version to be worse.
sentences.zh: 我现在预计新版本会更糟。

blocks.html_en: This isn’t a rant against AI. Those humming GPU farms have given us superpowers, but we still can’t use them to build better software.

sentences.en: This isn’t a rant against AI.
sentences.zh: 这并不是对人工智能的咆哮。

sentences.en: Those humming GPU farms have given us superpowers, but we still can’t use them to build better software.
sentences.zh: 那些嗡嗡作响的 GPU 农场赋予了我们超能力，但我们仍然无法使用它们来构建更好的软件。

sentences.en: Software vendors have long been KPI-oriented, and making things more stable doesn’t always have a direct effect on the numbers.
sentences.zh: 软件供应商长期以来一直以 KPI 为导向，让事情变得更加稳定并不总是对数字产生直接影响。

sentences.en: It doesn’t look exciting in presentations:
sentences.zh: 演示中看起来并不令人兴奋：

blocks.html_en:

sentences.en: — Imaginary PM at a BigCo
sentences.zh: — 某大型公司的想象中的 PM

blocks.html_en: Until this attitude changes, the great software quality decay will continue.

sentences.en: Until this attitude changes, the great software quality decay will continue.
sentences.zh: 在这种态度改变之前，软件质量的严重衰退将继续下去。

blocks.html_en: That doesn’t sound optimistic, but I’m actually excited about what comes next. As companies collectively spiral into AI debt, individual developers have a unique opportunity to build software that would previously have been beyond their reach.

sentences.en: That doesn’t sound optimistic, but I’m actually excited about what comes next.
sentences.zh: 这听起来并不乐观，但我实际上对接下来发生的事情感到兴奋。

sentences.en: As companies collectively spiral into AI debt, individual developers have a unique opportunity to build software that would previously have been beyond their reach.
sentences.zh: 随着公司集体陷入人工智能债务，个人开发者拥有了独特的机会来构建以前超出他们能力范围的软件。

blocks.html_en: I have no hope for my car’s Android Auto or LG’s stupid website – but I choose to believe that everyday software will get better as a result of this frustration. We’re already seeing acts of rebellion against the current state of macOS and Windows, and I hope the trend will spread across the stack.

sentences.en: I have no hope for my car’s Android Auto or LG’s stupid website – but I choose to believe that everyday software will get better as a result of this frustration.
sentences.zh: 我对我车上的 Android Auto 或 LG 愚蠢的网站不抱希望，但我选择相信日常软件会因为这种挫败感而变得更好。

sentences.en: We’re already seeing acts of rebellion against the current state of macOS and Windows, and I hope the trend will spread across the stack.
sentences.zh: 我们已经看到了针对 macOS 和 Windows 当前状态的反抗行为，我希望这种趋势能够在整个堆栈中蔓延。

content_html_zh:
当我写这篇文章时，我们正处于人工智能引发的大规模精神病之中。 人们实际上是在把自己塞进医院病床上，争先恐后地在一切都被自动化之前夺取一些市场价值。 我不能责怪他们。 模型不断变得更好，程序员左右被解雇。 我们多次被告知，到今年年底，人工智能将编写 100% 的代码。 无论这是否属实，现在可能都不是袖手旁观的最佳时机。

代理时代的广泛兴奋伴随着更高生产力和更高质量的承诺。 不可否认，这些新工具已经彻底改变了我们创建和使用软件的方式。 他们提高了高层管理人员对团队产出的期望。 他们可能以我们以前从未见过的方式提升了软件团队的平均技能。

那么，为什么软件的整体性能不断恶化呢？

仅上周的几个例子：

  • 我的银行应用程序平均需要 3 次 FaceID 登录才能出现 3D 安全确认视图。
  • 我在 macOS 上打开 Slack，图标在扩展坞中持续弹跳几秒钟。 我不耐烦了，切换到 Ghostty，开始打字。 就在这时，Slack 窗口出现了，抢走了 Ghostty 的焦点，并且 git pull 命令被发送到群聊中。
  • 我的 LG 冰箱开始发出奇怪的声音，因此我尝试通过包含无数字段的多步骤表格提交保修索赔。 最后因提交错误而失败。 我是因为查看了 JavaScript 控制台才知道的。
  • 我汽车的信息娱乐系统最近进行了软件更新。 一开始它就不是很好，但至少它不会在每次驾驶过程中自行重新启动。 现在，它充满了错误：转向灯的声音随机变得安静，直到我重新启动操作系统；我点击屏幕打开 Google 地图 – 收音机应用程序出现；点击屏幕后，有 1-2 秒的延迟才会发生任何事情。 此时，这不再只是一个用户体验问题 - 这些错误会影响您专注于驾驶的能力。 几个月前，我在 LinkedIn 上看到了重新设计汽车操作系统的团队的一位产品经理的帖子。 他们祝贺自己所做的工作多么出色。 每次我不得不与他们的产品作斗争时，我都会想起那篇文章。

虽然我无法了解完整的故事，但我愿意打赌，这些错误背后的大多数团队都可以使用最新的模型，并拥有慷慨的代币预算。 如果有机会的话，法学硕士可以非常擅长消除错误。

软件总是有缺陷的，对一切都很稳定的 macOS Snow Leopard 时代的怀念主要是选择性记忆的产物。 过去的软件可能更好，但这主要是因为它更简单。 从那时起，我们不断提出新的抽象、新的前端框架和更多的基础设施复杂性。 “用户体验”的门槛不断提高，但一切都变得越来越脆弱。

我们已经到了这样一个地步：macOS（或者我真正依赖的任何应用程序）的更新让人感到恐惧而不是兴奋。 我现在预计新版本会更糟。

这并不是对人工智能的咆哮。 那些嗡嗡作响的 GPU 农场赋予了我们超能力，但我们仍然无法使用它们来构建更好的软件。

软件供应商长期以来一直以 KPI 为导向，让事情变得更加稳定并不总是对数字产生直接影响。 演示中看起来并不令人兴奋：

本季度，我们不会发布任何新功能，也没有重新设计任何内容的计划 - 我们将专注于修复错误。 — 某大型公司的想象中的 PM

在这种态度改变之前，软件质量的严重衰退将继续下去。

这听起来并不乐观，但我实际上对接下来发生的事情感到兴奋。 随着公司集体陷入人工智能债务，个人开发者拥有了独特的机会来构建以前超出他们能力范围的软件。

我对我车上的 Android Auto 或 LG 愚蠢的网站不抱希望，但我选择相信日常软件会因为这种挫败感而变得更好。 我们已经看到了针对 macOS 和 Windows 当前状态的反抗行为，我希望这种趋势能够在整个堆栈中蔓延。
